article_title: JWST Solves Saturn's Decades-Long Rotation Mystery, Revealing a Self-Sustaining Auroral Heat Pump in the Upper Atmosphere

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: JWST Solves Saturn's Decades-Long Rotation Mystery, Revealing a Self-Sustaining Auroral Heat Pump in the Upper Atmosphere
content_preview.summary: James Webb observations show Saturn's apparent spin change is driven by a feedback loop in which its aurora heats the atmosphere, generating winds and currents that sustain the aurora.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Astronomers say they have resolved a decades-long puzzle over why Saturn appears to change its rotation rate, concluding that the planet's spin was never actually shifting. Using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), a team led by Professor Tom Stallard of Northumbria University found that the apparent change is produced by winds high in Saturn's atmosphere, which are themselves driven by the planet's aurora in a self-sustaining cycle, according to [Northumbria University](https://...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-paced News piece (504 words) explaining a non-trivial planetary-science result — the auroral 'heat pump' feedback loop — in accessible terms.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200; phys.org via Archive.org fallback, content intact). source-3 (Northumbria) confirms the Tom Stallard lead, NIRSpec, the northern auroral region over a Saturnian day, the trihydrogen-cation 'natural thermometer', and all seven collaborator institutions (Boston University, Leicester, Aberystwyth, Reading, Imperial College London, Lancaster, Johns Hopkins APL). source-0 (phys.org) confirms the 'something strange was happening' Stallard quote and the Journal of Geophysical Research: Space Physics publication. source-4 (Newsweek) confirms the 'planetary heat pump … the system feeds itself' quote, 'ten times' accuracy, and the ~50-degree prior uncertainty. source-2 (ScienceDaily) confirms the 2004 Cassini measurements and the 2021 winds hypothesis. source-1 (SciTechDaily) confirms the 'changes how we think about planetary atmospheres more generally' quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every direct quote is verbatim and correctly attributed to Stallard via the cited outlet. The parenthetical '(H3+)' is the standard chemical formula for the verbatim-named 'trihydrogen cation', not a fabricated specific. 'Distorting/being distorted' is a faithful paraphrase (outside quote marks) of ScienceDaily's 'altered the auroral signal' / 'being affected by winds'. All dates and numbers trace to snapshots.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; clearly separates established findings from open questions.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior Saturn/JWST rotation coverage in the archive.
recommendations: Added newsroom.northumbria.ac.uk to the allowlist as the lead institution's official newsroom.
